prompt: Five translucent mountain-shaped silhouettes of people, arranged vertically, each representing a country's income distribution. The leftmost and smallest mountain — Ethiopia — is almost entirely submerged below a dark rising waterline. The rightmost mountain — the United States — towers far above the waterline, massive and glowing warm gold. No text, no labels, no axes, no legend. Single frame. Cinematic, painterly, editorial magazine style.
dataset_name: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform via Our World in Data
insight: Ethiopia's national poverty line of $2.59/day sits below what the US considers extreme poverty — the same word describes incomparably different realities across five countries.
concept: income distribution inequality across countries visualized as human figures at different water levels
tradition_approach: Emotional metaphor using water submersion to represent populations living below poverty lines
